name: Támara de Campos from Frómista — circular through the Tierra de Campos
name_original: Támara de Campos desde Frómista — circular por la Tierra de Campos
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Frómista are characterized by the flat, undulating terrain of the Tierra de Campos region, featuring extensive agricultural landscapes. The area offers long, straight stretches suitable for consistent riding, with gentle uphill slopes to the east. A prominent feature is the historic Canal de Castilla, which provides scenic sections for cycling. The Camino de Santiago also passes through Frómista, offering routes that combine historical significance with open plains. Best road cycling routes around Frómista The most popular road cycling route is Fromista from Palencia – circular by Castillo de Sarmiento and Astudillo, a 67.0 miles (107.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 18 minutes to complete, passing through historical towns. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Osorno la Mayor from Carrión de los Condes – circular by Fromista and the rivers Valdivia and Carrión, a moderate 37.0 miles (59.5 km) path that includes sections along the Valdivia and Carrión rivers. Local road cyclists also love the Torquemada from Astudillo – circular by Castillo de la Mota and Valdeolmillos, a 34.0 miles (54.8 km) trail leading through agricultural plains and past historical sites, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Road cycling around Frómista is defined by flat, undulating terrain, extensive agricultural landscapes, and the Canal de Castilla. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Frómista are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from 2 reviews. More than 50 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Frómista's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Frómista?
A: There are 5 road cycling routes around Frómista, offering a variety of experiences across the region's flat and undulating terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Frómista?
A: The terrain around Frómista is characterized by flat, undulating plains within the Tierra de Campos region, featuring extensive agricultural landscapes. You'll find long, straight stretches ideal for consistent riding, with gentle uphill slopes primarily to the east. The historic Canal de Castilla also offers scenic, flat sections.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Frómista?
A: Yes, Frómista offers 3 easy road cycling routes. An excellent option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243354575/tamara-de-campos-from-fromista-circular-through-the-tierra-de-campos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Támara de Campos from Frómista — circular through the Tierra de Campos&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.5 miles (21.8 km) long with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on road cycling routes in Frómista?
A: Given the region's generally flat and undulating nature, elevation gains are moderate. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1158326535/fromista-from-palencia-circular-by-castillo-de-sarmiento-and-astudillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fromista from Palencia – circular by Castillo de Sarmiento and Astudillo&lt;/a&gt; route, which is 67.0 miles (107.7 km), has an elevation gain of approximately 530 meters.

Q: What natural features can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes incorporate the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, an 18th-century engineering marvel. You can also see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quadruple Lock of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; in Frómista itself. Some routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159984642/osorno-la-mayor-from-carrion-de-los-condes-circular-by-fromista-and-the-rivers-valdivia-and-carrion" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osorno la Mayor from Carrión de los Condes&lt;/a&gt;, also include sections along the Valdivia and Carrión rivers.

Q: Are there historical landmarks or cultural stops along the routes?
A: Yes, Frómista is a significant stop on the Camino de Santiago, and many routes pass through or near historical towns and sites. You can visit the famous Romanesque Church of San Martín in &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4541372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frómista&lt;/a&gt;, or pass by historical sites like Castillo de la Mota on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170729077/torquemada-from-astudillo-circular-by-castillo-de-la-mota-and-valdeolmillos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torquemada from Astudillo&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Frómista?
A: The sweeping Castilian plains offer wide-open spaces and big skies, making spring and autumn particularly pleasant for cycling. The Canal de Castilla sections can also provide a cooling effect during warmer months. Avoid the peak heat of summer afternoons.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, most of the road cycling routes around Frómista are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38149028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frómista – Lock number 21 loop from Frómista&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Frómista?
A: The road cycling routes in Frómista are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.5 stars from 2 reviews. Over 50 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the quiet agricultural landscapes and historical connections.

Q: Is Frómista a good destination for family-friendly road cycling?
A: The generally flat and undulating terrain, especially along the Canal de Castilla, makes Frómista suitable for families. Easy routes with minimal elevation are available, providing a relaxed cycling experience through scenic agricultural areas.

Q: Is it possible to find cafes or places to refuel along the routes?
A: As Frómista is a key stop on the Camino de Santiago, you'll find various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in the town itself and in other villages along the Camino. These provide convenient stops for refueling during your ride.
tips: The church of San Hipólito el Real in Támara de Campo is an impressive temple built in the 14th century. Its imposing structure stands out in the landscape of Tierra de Campos. With Gothic and Renaissance architectural elements, its construction took place for more than a hundred years, ending in the 16th century.

Frómista is a town with about 800 inhabitants and all kinds of services.
Here the itineraries of the Camino de Santiago and the Canal de Castilla intersect, so that it is a place very frequented by pilgrims.
Be sure to visit the Romanesque church of San Martín de Tours, after all you are in Palencia and this province is famous for the Romanesque it houses.
At this point there is a public fountain and, very close, several bars and ATMs.

waypoints.name: Church of San Hipólito el Real of Támara de Campos
